ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "tweet_text" TEXT /* Text of the tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the tweet was posted */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "is_quote" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a quote */,
  "is_reply" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a reply to another tweet */,
  "reply_to_tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet being replied to */,
  "reply_to_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being replied to */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* List of hashtags used in the tweet */,
  "mentions" TEXT /* List of user mentions in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet the metric belongs to */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of quotes */,
  "impression_count" INTEGER /* Number of impressions the tweet received */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate of the tweet */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Calculated engagement score based on various metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "coding" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each coding */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet the coding belongs to */,
  "code" INTEGER /* Code assigned to the tweet */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the code */,
  "coding_source" TEXT /* Source of the coding (e.g., manual, NLP model) */,
  "coding_date" TEXT /* Date when the coding was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_coding_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet the sentiment analysis belongs to */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score (-1 to 1) */,
  "sentiment_type" TEXT /* Type of sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_source" TEXT /* Source of the sentiment analysis (e.g., manual, NLP model) */,
  "sentiment_date" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment analysis was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_teams" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the research team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the research team */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the research team was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the research team was last updated */,
  "lead_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the lead user of the research team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_research_teams_lead_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("lead_user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_articles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each news article */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the news article */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the news article */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the news article */,
  "published_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the news article was published */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the news article (e.g., newspaper, website) */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the news article */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the news article */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "government_reports" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each government report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the government report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the government report */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the government report */,
  "published_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the government report was published */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the government report (e.g., government agency) */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the government report */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the government report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_news_article" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet */,
  "news_article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the news article */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_government_report" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet */,
  "government_report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the government report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_research_team" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "research_team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the research team */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  "is_lead" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is the lead of the research team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permission" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_team_news_article" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "research_team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the research team */,
  "news_article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the news article */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_team_government_report" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "research_team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the research team */,
  "government_report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the government report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
